title: Power-conditioning selection bias in cross-database pharmacovigilance replication (FAERS-DAEN): analysis and simulation code
description: Reproducibility code for the simulation-led study "Power-conditioning selection bias in cross-database pharmacovigilance replication: a simulation study of the reference-negative enrichment test, with a pre-registered FAERS-DAEN case study." The primary contribution is a simulation of the reference-negative enrichment test across a 126-cell data-generating grid (2,000 replicates per cell, six power-conditioning selection rules, four cluster-aware estimators), reporting type-I error, power, and few-clusters confidence-interval coverage, with four pre-registered hypotheses (S1-S4) graded honest-either-way. The simulation runs from a clean clone with no external data (fitted marginals are baked into sim_config.yaml). The repository also reproduces the demoted empirical FAERS-DAEN case study: the per-pair 2x2 disproportionality pipeline, the minimum-detectable-effect (MDE) power model, the ground-truth reference-negative enrichment test, and all sensitivity arms and figures.
